title: lupinus leucophyllus subsp. leucophyllus
intro: Lupinus leucophyllus subsp. leucophyllus, also known as Big Bend lupine, is a perennial plant species in the genus Lupinus that is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to the presence of various alkaloids. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to support respiratory health and reduce symptoms of anxiety and insomnia due to its sedative and expectorant properties. Lupinus leucophyllus subsp. leucophyllus can be prepared into herbal remedies such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects on human health.
